findings.description: The lighthouse panoramic image and cityscape panoramic image in the HTML are smaller and stacked differently than in the PDF title slide, but both are present and in the correct section.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two panoramic images spanning the full width as background-style elements; HTML shows them as smaller inline images stacked vertically.

findings.description: The title slide layout in the PDF has the NC logo on the left with the title text and author info on the right in a side-by-side arrangement. The HTML renders everything in a single column stacked layout.
findings.evidence: PDF: NC logo left-aligned with title and author info to the right. HTML: NC logo above, then title text, then author info, all stacked vertically.

findings.description: The coal combustion image on slide 2 is present in both PDF and HTML but rendered at a different size in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a larger image to the right of the bullet list; HTML shows a smaller version below the bullet list.

findings.description: Slide 2 has a two-region layout in the PDF (bullets on left, image on right). HTML collapses this to a single column with bullets above and image below.
findings.evidence: PDF: Purpose, Methodology, Major Components bullets on left, coal image on right. HTML: bullets stacked above the image.

findings.description: The Regulatory Impact Analysis data on slide 7 is presented as a simple list in the PDF but rendered as a proper table in the HTML. Both contain the same data and are readable.
findings.evidence: PDF: label-value pairs in aligned text. HTML: structured table with Category and Impact columns.

findings.description: The Questions and Answers contact information on slide 8 is presented as inline text in the PDF but rendered as a table with Name/Email/Phone columns in the HTML. Data is the same.
findings.evidence: PDF: Andrew Brooks and Daniel Kang contact info in text lines. HTML: structured table with Name, Email, Phone headers.

findings.description: The Regulatory Impact Analysis on page 7 is presented as a simple list of label-value pairs in the PDF, not as a formal table with headers. The HTML converts this into a table with fabricated column headers 'Category' and 'Impact' that do not appear in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF page 7 shows a simple label-value layout (e.g., 'State Government: Minimal Costs'). HTML adds <thead> with <th>Category</th> and <th>Impact</th> which are not in the source.

findings.description: The contact information on page 8 is presented as aligned text in the PDF, not as a formal table. The HTML converts this into a table with fabricated column headers 'Name', 'Email', and 'Phone' that do not appear in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF page 8 shows contact info as aligned text lines. HTML creates a table with <th>Name</th>, <th>Email</th>, <th>Phone</th> headers that don't exist in source.

findings.description: The hyperlinks from the PDF are preserved in text but not rendered as clickable links in the HTML. The URLs are present as plain text rather than <a> tags.
findings.evidence: PDF page 8 shows clickable links for 'https://deq.nc.gov/documents/15a-ncac-02k-0224...' and email addresses. HTML has these as plain text strings without <a href> wrappers.

findings.description: Two consecutive sections (pages 6 and 7) both use the same h2 heading 'Major Components of Dam Safety CCR Rule', which is accurate to the PDF but page 7's content is primarily about Regulatory Impact Analysis, which is rendered as an h3 under that h2. This is a reasonable structural choice.
findings.evidence: Pages 6 and 7 of PDF both have the title 'Major Components of Dam Safety CCR Rule'. HTML correctly reflects this.

findings.description: The HTML contains two nested <main> elements, which is a structural duplication issue in the HTML markup, though it doesn't affect content display.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> inside <body>, then another <main> inside the first <main>.
